# T3 Code Ultralight Browser Fork

Ultralight localhost bridge, headless client, and embeddable chat UI for a user's authenticated local Codex.

- Machine-readable integration contract: ./integration.json
- One-command verified recipe: run the release CLI with `setup --mode iframe|react|element|custom --allow-origin <exact browser origin> --json`
- Run setup from the host project root; its normalized directory becomes the bridge default, with `--cwd` as an override
- Bridge reuse requires the exact version, origin policy, workspace fingerprint, and Codex-binary fingerprint; browser status never exposes either path
- Custom Codex CLI: pass the same `--codex PATH` to setup, start, or serve; diagnostics and the background app-server both use it
- Durable host startup: use the setup receipt's structured `lifecycle.ensure.installed` or `lifecycle.ensure.zeroInstall` command before the web process; it restores the exact resolved port, workspace, binary, and origins
- Zero-install browser delivery: add `--delivery hosted` for element or custom modes
- Custom UI primary API: import `createCodexAssistant` from `/codex-assistant.js` or `t3-code-ultralight-browser-fork/assistant`; it owns stateful threads, cancellation, scoped fail-closed requests, and cleanup
- Complete-chat host control: `codex-chat.sendPrompt()`, React imperative ref, or `/codex-embed.js` for raw iframes
- Packed framework verification: React 18/19, Next.js 16 App Router, Vue 3, and Svelte 5; the React entry declares its client boundary, while custom-element mount attributes initialize one frame and pre-ready controls wait safely
- Host commands: send, stop, and newThread; exact parent window and bridge-allowed Origin required; acknowledgements never contain prompt or response text
- Browser hosts: pass exact `--allow-origin` even on localhost; only the bridge's own UI origin is automatic, and unlisted sibling loopback pages are rejected
- Trusted file or sandbox hosts: pass `--allow-origin null` and require `opaqueOriginAllowed: true`
- Complete chat budget: 110000 decoded JS+CSS bytes; built-in safe Markdown has no external runtime dependency
- Agent implementation guide: ./docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md
- Human quickstart and API overview: ./README.md
- Detailed recipes and security model: ./docs/INTEGRATION.md
- Public repository: https://github.com/adamholter/t3-code-ultralight-browser-fork
- Canonical release assets: clean-tag rebuilt, checked, audited, npm-dry-run verified, and GitHub provenance-attested by .github/workflows/release.yml

Prefer the complete isolated chat unless the host already owns a custom canvas, voice, spatial, game, or bespoke interface. Keep the bridge loopback-only, preserve approvals, and verify one real Codex turn through the final UI.
